title: [cross-file] iface_init_failed not checked in lichen_peer_add/remove
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:lichen_peer_add/remove <-> lichen_l2.c:iface_init_failed

Bug: lichen_peer_add() (line 599) and lichen_peer_remove() (line 702) check lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit() and lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() but do NOT check atomic_get(&iface_init_failed).

iface_init_failed is set when lichen_l2_iface_init() fails partway through (lines 1355, 1370, 1387, 1478, 1545). After this failure:
- link_ctx may be partially initialized or cleaned up
- peer_table may be in inconsistent state

lichen_peer_add/remove proceed to modify peer_table even if init failed. While this might not crash (peer_table is static), it creates inconsistent state where peers are added to a non-functional interface.

Compare: lichen_l2_send() (line 820), lichen_l2_enable() (line 964), and lora_rx_callback() (line 1299) all check iface_init_failed.

Trigger:
1. Interface initialization fails (e.g., link_local derivation fails at line 1500)
2. iface_init_failed is set to 1
3. Application calls lichen_peer_add() to add a peer
4. No iface_init_failed check, function proceeds
5. Peer added to peer_table of non-functional interface
close_reason: Already fixed: lichen_peer_add() checks iface_init_failed at line 625-629 and lichen_peer_remove() checks at line 742-746. Both return -ENODEV if init failed. Code matches the fix described in the issue.
